    33	def get_fixed_timezone(offset):
    34	    """Return a tzinfo instance with a fixed offset from UTC."""
    35	    if isinstance(offset, timedelta):
    36	        offset = offset.total_seconds() // 60
    37	    sign = '-' if offset < 0 else '+'
    38	    hhmm = '%02d%02d' % divmod(abs(offset), 60)
    39	    name = sign + hhmm
    40	    return timezone(timedelta(minutes=offset), name)

   112	class override(ContextDecorator):
   113	    """
   114	    Temporarily set the time zone for the current thread.
   115	
   116	    This is a context manager that uses django.utils.timezone.activate()
   117	    to set the timezone on entry and restores the previously active timezone
   118	    on exit.
   119	
   120	    The ``timezone`` argument must be an instance of a ``tzinfo`` subclass, a
   121	    time zone name, or ``None``. If it is ``None``, Django enables the default
   122	    time zone.
   123	    """
   124	    def __init__(self, timezone):
   125	        self.timezone = timezone
   126	
   127	    def __enter__(self):
   128	        self.old_timezone = getattr(_active, 'value', None)
   129	        if self.timezone is None:
   130	            deactivate()
   131	        else:
   132	            activate(self.timezone)
   133	
   134	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):
   135	        if self.old_timezone is None:
   136	            deactivate()
   137	        else:
   138	            _active.value = self.old_timezone

   143	def template_localtime(value, use_tz=None):
   144	    """
   145	    Check if value is a datetime and converts it to local time if necessary.
   146	
   147	    If use_tz is provided and is not None, that will force the value to
   148	    be converted (or not), overriding the value of settings.USE_TZ.
   149	
   150	    This function is designed for use by the template engine.
   151	    """
   152	    should_convert = (
   153	        isinstance(value, datetime) and
   154	        (settings.USE_TZ if use_tz is None else use_tz) and
   155	        not is_naive(value) and
   156	        getattr(value, 'convert_to_local_time', True)
   157	    )
   158	    return localtime(value) if should_convert else value

   163	def localtime(value=None, timezone=None):
   164	    """
   165	    Convert an aware datetime.datetime to local time.
   166	
   167	    Only aware datetimes are allowed. When value is omitted, it defaults to
   168	    now().
   169	
   170	    Local time is defined by the current time zone, unless another time zone
   171	    is specified.
   172	    """
   173	    if value is None:
   174	        value = now()
   175	    if timezone is None:
   176	        timezone = get_current_timezone()
   177	    # Emulate the behavior of astimezone() on Python < 3.6.
   178	    if is_naive(value):
   179	        raise ValueError("localtime() cannot be applied to a naive datetime")
   180	    return value.astimezone(timezone)

   188	    now().
   189

   191	    specified.

   207	    """
   208	    Determine if a given datetime.datetime is aware.
   209	
   210	    The concept is defined in Python's docs:
   211	    https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.tzinfo
   212	
   213	    Assuming value.tzinfo is either None or a proper datetime.tzinfo,
   214	    value.utcoffset() implements the appropriate logic.
   215	    """

   220	    """
   221	    Determine if a given datetime.datetime is naive.
   222	
   223	    The concept is defined in Python's docs:
   224	    https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.tzinfo
   225	
   226	    Assuming value.tzinfo is either None or a proper datetime.tzinfo,
   227	    value.utcoffset() implements the appropriate logic.
   228	    """
   229	    return value.utcoffset() is None

   232	def make_aware(value, timezone=None, is_dst=None):
   233	    """Make a naive datetime.datetime in a given time zone aware."""
   234	    if timezone is None:
   235	        timezone = get_current_timezone()
   236	    if _is_pytz_zone(timezone):
   237	        # This method is available for pytz time zones.
   238	        return timezone.localize(value, is_dst=is_dst)
   239	    else:
   240	        # Check that we won't overwrite the timezone of an aware datetime.
   241	        if is_aware(value):
   242	            raise ValueError(
   243	                "make_aware expects a naive datetime, got %s" % value)

   245	        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone)

   253	    if is_naive(value):

    38	def do_timezone(value, arg):
    39	    """
    40	    Convert a datetime to local time in a given time zone.
    41	
    42	    The argument must be an instance of a tzinfo subclass or a time zone name.
    43	
    44	    Naive datetimes are assumed to be in local time in the default time zone.
    45	    """
    46	    if not isinstance(value, datetime):
    47	        return ''
    48	
    49	    # Obtain a timezone-aware datetime
    50	    try:
    51	        if timezone.is_naive(value):
    52	            default_timezone = timezone.get_default_timezone()
    53	            value = timezone.make_aware(value, default_timezone)
    54	    # Filters must never raise exceptions, and pytz' exceptions inherit
    55	    # Exception directly, not a specific subclass. So catch everything.
    56	    except Exception:
    57	        return ''
    58	
    59	    # Obtain a tzinfo instance
    60	    if isinstance(arg, tzinfo):
    61	        tz = arg
    62	    elif isinstance(arg, str):
    63	        try:
    64	            tz = pytz.timezone(arg)
    65	        except pytz.UnknownTimeZoneError:
    66	            return ''
    67	    else:
    68	        return ''

    71	
    72	    # HACK: the convert_to_local_time flag will prevent
    73	    #       automatic conversion of the value to local time.
    74	    result = datetimeobject(result.year, result.month, result.day,
    75	                            result.hour, result.minute, result.second,
    76	                            result.microsecond, result.tzinfo)

   427	    if dt is None:
   428	        return None
   429	    try:
   430	        dt = backend_utils.typecast_timestamp(dt)
   431	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
   432	        return None
   433	    if conn_tzname:
   434	        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=pytz.timezone(conn_tzname))
   435	    if tzname is not None and tzname != conn_tzname:
   436	        sign_index = tzname.find('+') + tzname.find('-') + 1
   437	        if sign_index > -1:
   438	            sign = tzname[sign_index]
   439	            tzname, offset = tzname.split(sign)
   440	            if offset:
   441	                hours, minutes = offset.split(':')
   442	                offset_delta = datetime.timedelta(hours=int(hours), minutes=int(minutes))
   443	                dt += offset_delta if sign == '+' else -offset_delta
   444	        dt = timezone.localtime(dt, pytz.timezone(tzname))
   445	    return dt

        try:

            ExpectedException = ZoneInfoNotFoundError
        except ImportError:
            from backports.zoneinfo import ZoneInfoNotFoundError
            ExpectedException = ZoneInfoNotFoundError
        with self.assertRaises(ExpectedException):
            Template("{% load tz %}{% timezone tz %}{% endtimezone %}").render(Context({'tz': 'foobar'}))
